_description: Base-rate approval probabilities (Phase 3 → FDA approval) by indication/sub-indication. Used by pre_phase3_readout_scanner to seed the Approval Probability dimension of the binary_catalyst rubric. Rates are conditional on hitting the Phase 3 primary endpoint. Sources in _provenance. Values are the mid-point of published ranges; conservative floor is used where sources disagree.
_provenance: BIO, Biomedtracker, Amplion 2011-2020 'Clinical Development Success Rates and Contributing Factors' (published 2021)
Hay et al. 2014 Nature Biotechnology — Phase 3 to approval rates by therapeutic area
Wong, Siah, Lo 2019 — MIT CSAIL updated success-rate tables
Mullard 2023 Nature Reviews Drug Discovery — recent trends in Phase 3 to approval
Conservative floor applied where datasets disagree; rare-disease rates capped downward by 5pp to account for confirmatory-study risk
_scoring_hint: For the `binary_catalyst.approval_probability` dimension (0-5, weighted ×2.5): rate ≥0.75 → 5, 0.65-0.75 → 4, 0.55-0.65 → 3, 0.45-0.55 → 2, <0.45 → 1. Adjust downward if trial has prior failed Phase 3 in same indication.

oncology_solid_tumor.notes: Highly variable — front-line vs. later-line; IO combos trending higher

oncology_hematologic.notes: CAR-T and bispecifics pulling rates higher in recent years

oncology_rare.notes: Rare-tumor pathway with accelerated approval common

cardiovascular.notes: Large trials, high placebo response; MACE endpoints tough

metabolic_diabetes.notes: GLP-1 class has pulled the average up meaningfully

neurology_alzheimers.notes: Historically the graveyard; recent anti-amyloid approvals shift base slightly

psychiatry_depression.notes: High placebo response is the killer

gastro_nash.notes: Multiple high-profile failures; resmetirom precedent now

ophthalmology_rare.notes: Thyroid eye disease, DED; recent veligrotug/tepezza precedent

pain_chronic.notes: Endpoint validation and placebo response challenges

default.notes: Use as fallback when indication not in table
